config.subject: {{form.Salesforce_Opportunity_Name}} Electronic Export Information
config.plainTextBody:
Hi {{form.Integration_POC_First_Name}},

Your shipments are required to include an Electronic Export Information (EEI) filing because they meet one or more of the following conditions:

- A single item (by HS code) exceeds $2,500 USD in value and is not going to Canada
- The commodity requires an export license (due to the item and/or destination country)
- The shipment is destined for China, Russia, or Venezuela, regardless of value

Exceptions may apply. For additional details, please review the following resource:
https://zonos.com/docs/global-ecommerce/shipping-labels/electronic-export-information

widgets.content:
Process when Zonos is used in multiple shipping profiles

  • Shopify sends an API request for each profile
  • Zonos sends a quote for each request
  • Shopify combines the quotes for a single amount
    • Zonos does not have insight into how Shopify combines these quotes
  • If order is completed, Zonos recalculates into a single quote 
    • A variance is impossible to avoid in these scenarios
    • Using cartonization with this will also pose issues
      • ie - two profiles will produce two API calls with more cartons than the final Zonos quote

tasks.name: Magento - Install the Zonos Checkout app

widgets.content:
Steps:

  1. In order to use Checkout with Magento, you need to purchase the extension from the Magento Marketplace, then install it in your terminal with Composer. This approach is more technical than our other plugins due to the way that Magento handles extensions. To install the app:

    1. Download the Checkout extension from the Magento Marketplace.
    2. Download the Hello extension from the Magento Marketplace.
    3. Open a new terminal and navigate to the root directory of your Magento installation

For the merchant:

  1. Go to your Magento admin -> Stores -> Configuration -> General.
  2. In the Allow countries section, highlight all countries that you want to ship to, internationally and domestically. If a country is not highlighted here, but is enabled in Zonos, the order will be allowed to complete and will appear in Dashboard, but it will fail to import to Magento.
  3. Select every country except for the United States in the Zip/postal code is optional for section. This reduces errors in completing orders since some countries do not use or require postal codes. Additionally, because Checkout comes with Zonos' fraud protection, you will not incur additional risk for removing this requirement.

For onboarding:

tasks.name: Shopify - Install the Zonos Duty and Tax app

widgets.content: There are two steps needed to complete the Zonos installation in Shopify. First, the Zonos app will need to be downloaded. Second, the platform subscription will need to be accepted. Follow the steps outlined below. Complete this task once done.

Steps:

  1. Download Zonos app from the Shopify app store.
  2. Click Install

  3. Copy your credentials from your Zonos account (settings -> integrations)

    1. Zonos account number
    2. Rest API key
    3. Zonos API
  4. In Shopify, paste your Zonos credentials
  5. Click save
  6. Select subscription type (reference on Zonos order form)
  7. Click approve
  8. Do not enable the Zonos app yet

Note: If you don't have a Zonos credential token listed in the Zonos Dashboard, contact your project manager - {{form.Project_Manager_(First_Name)}}

After installing the Zonos Duty and Tax App, please grant collaborator access in Shopify to your project manager.
Note that requests are usually sent within 24 hours of app installation.

The Zonos Duty and Tax app requires the enablement of Shopify's Carrier Service API to successfully rate a fully landed cost in the checkout. If you are unsure if this feature is enabled please reach out to Shopify support for confirmation.

tasks.name: ⚖️ Add physical weight to Shopify SKUs

widgets.content:
Zonos uses the physical weight value set at the product level to calculate the shipping costs of a shipment. To ensure carrier-calculated shipping rates are accurate in the checkout, we recommend adding a physical weight value to all active Shopify SKUs. 

Active SKUs that are missing a physical weight value in the product detail will rate carrier-calcualted shipping rates inaccurately.

How to Edit and Update Product Weights in Shopify

  1. In your Shopify admin, go to Products -> All products.
  2. Click the title of the product that you want to add weight to.
  3. Scroll down to the Shipping section.
    1. If the product has variants, you'll need to click one of the variants in the Variant section to see the Shipping section.
  4. Enter the weight under Weight.
  5. Click Save.

Instructions for creating custom messages

  1. From your Zonos dashboard, go to settings > custom messages (under checkout)
  2. Click Add custom message
  3. Select page (location of messages appear on the left, unless default, which displays on the right)
    1. Default - displays on every checkout page
    2. Customer information
    3. Shipping
    4. Payment
  4. Add custom message
  5. Select country/countries
  6. Save

Possible use-cases

  • Display link for domestic checkout with custom messages for domestic customers incorrectly directed to Zonos checkout
  • Setting customer expectation
  • Explaining return policies

Managing message translations

When you create a custom message, it will be automatically translated into all supported languages.

Once you have created a custom message, it will go into a translation queue and be translated within a few minutes. The message will be grayed-out in the list until the translation is complete. To view the translated version of the message, hover over the message to reveal the More options (•••) menu, then select View translations.

If you wish to decline any of the automatic translations, you can click the "thumbs down" button next to the translation. This will remove the translation from being displayed as well as providing feedback to our translation system.

tasks.name: Add product details to Zonos Catalog

widgets.content:
The Zonos catalog can be used to manage dimensional weight rating, country-level product restrictions, and customs information (HS code and Country of Origin). If you would like Zonos to manage those aspects of your catalog, fill out the attached file and upload below.

Template examples:

  • Add Dimensional Weight: column A, column H, I, J
  • Add HS code(s): column A, column O
  • Add Country of Origin: column A, column N
  • Add Country Restrictions(s): column A, column M (2 digit ISO country code required)

tasks.name: Add product dimensions to Zonos Catalog

widgets.content:
Dimensional weight is determined by the carrier based on the length, width, and height of your packages.

If your dimensional weight is larger than your order's weight, then your customer is charged for the dimensional weight of their shipment. 

Uploading product dimensions to your Zonos catalog will ensure that shipping is quoted accurately at the checkout.

Required details:

  • Column A: SKU#

  • Column G: Dimensional unit (ounces, pounds, grams, and kilograms)

  • Column H: Length
  • Column I: Width
  • Column J: Height

Included in your yearly platform subscription are 10,000 product classifications. Bulk classification is a good way to obtain the 6-digit HS code for a large portion of your product catalog quickly. 

To ensure accurate classification, it is recommended to provide as many fields as possible.

  • The only required field to request a bulk classification is 'description'.
  • The template must be uploaded with the exact header columns; if a header is deleted, the upload will fail.

Enabling US sales tax requires two API calls

  1. Query for billingCompanyId
  2. Mutation to update merchant of record

Query for billingCompanyId

  • Open explore.zonos.com
  • Change store to your merchant's Zonos account
  • Copy their organizationId from the top of the screen
    • Top center of explore shows merchant name, merchant ID, and organizationId
  • Add organizationId to query below and run to obtain the billingCompanyId

{
  billingCompanyByOrganizationId(
    organizationId: "organizationId"
  ) {
    companyId
    name
    accounts {
      id
      type
    }
    country
    email
    id
    name
    createdAt
    createdBy
    id
    organizations {
      id
      name
    }
  }
}

Mutation to update merchant of record

  • Update mutation below with billingCompanyId obtained in the query above, countryCode (where they're based), and set ZONOS as the merchantOfRecord (both fields)
  • Run mutation

mutation MoRUpdate {
  billingCompanyUpdate(
    input: {
      id: "billingCompanyId"
      merchantOfRecordOverrides: {
        countryCode: US
        merchantOfRecord: ZONOS
      }
      merchantOfRecord: ZONOS
    }
  ) {
    merchantOfRecordOverrides {
      countryCode
      merchantOfRecord
    }
    merchantOfRecord
  }
}

tasks.name: Add zx prefix to order number

widgets.content:
Adding zx upstream

If you can add the zx prefix upstream (before orders reach ShipStation), you only need to configure your Label Messages to display the zx order number:

Label Messages configuration

  1. Access Label Messages settings: Navigate to Settings → Printing → Printing Setup, then click Document Options for Label
  2. Configure Label Messages: In the Label Messages section, you can define up to 3 custom messages that will appear as reference numbers on UPS labels. Label Messages only accept preset values like "Custom Field 1", "Order #", etc.
  3. Set your Messages to use Custom Fields: Set Message #1 to [Order #]. If you already have a #1, add a #2 or #3
  4. Save Settings to apply your configuration Once complete, ShipStation wil automatically put your order number on your label, which includes your configured zx prefix.

Adding zx in ShipStation:

If you can't add the zx prefix upstream (before orders reach ShipStation), you'll need to configure your Label Messages and Automation Rules to display the zx order number:

Step 1: Label Messages configuration

First, configure your Label Messages to use Custom Fields that will be populated by automation rules:

  1. Access Label Messages settings: Navigate to Settings → Printing → Printing Setup, then click Document Options for Label
  2. Configure Label Messages: In the Label Messages section, you can define up to 3 custom messages that will appear as reference numbers on UPS labels. Label Messages only accept preset values like "Custom Field 1", "Custom Field 2", etc.
  3. Set your Messages to use Custom Fields: Set Message #1 to [Custom Field #1]. If you already have a #1, add a #2 or #3
  4. Save Settings to apply your configuration

Automation Rules configuration

After setting up your new Label Message, you'll need an automation rule to populate your Custom Fields with the zx prefix and order data. Navigate to Settings → Automation → Automation Rules then click Create a Rule. Rule for Zonos UPS orders:

  1. Name: "Zonos UPS Unlock"
  2. Ensure Active Rule is toggled on
  3. Select Orders match these specific criteria
  4. Set criteria: "International Order" = "True" AND "Carrier" + "Includes" + "UPS"
  5. Set actions:
    • Action Type: Select "Set Custom Field 1..." (or 2 or 3 depending on your Label Messages)
    • Custom Field 1 (or 2 or 3): zx[order #] (this combines the zx prefix with the order number)
  6. Click Save Rule Once complete, ShipStation will automatically populate your Custom Field with the zx prefix and order number for international UPS orders, which will appear on your UPS labels. Important: The Label Message configuration in ShipStation is a global change that impacts all carriers. The automation rules approach allows UPS Unlock to work seamlessly for Zonos orders while maintaining your preferred Label Messages for other shipments.

Below is example code that Complemar used to get LCG certified labels and CI’s. This is not a one-size fits all solution. The “if” statements that trigger the “then” statements below will differ based on the particular merchant.

The below code can be used as a guide, but is not intended as a plug-and-play directive for the merchant/3PL’s ProShip admin.

Bolded are the areas that are likely to change based on the merchant.

 

 

ProShip Code

Your shipments are required to include an Electronic Export Information (EEI) filing because they meet one or more of the following conditions:

rawWidgets.content:
Process when Zonos is used in multiple shipping profiles

  • Shopify sends an API request for each profile
  • Zonos sends a quote for each request
  • Shopify combines the quotes for a single amount
    • Zonos does not have insight into how Shopify combines these quotes
  • If order is completed, Zonos recalculates into a single quote 
    • A variance is impossible to avoid in these scenarios
    • Using cartonization with this will also pose issues
      • ie - two profiles will produce two API calls with more cartons than the final Zonos quote

taskTemplate.name: Magento - Install the Zonos Checkout app

rawWidgets.content:
For the merchant:

  1. Go to your Magento admin -> Stores -> Configuration -> General.
  2. In the Allow countries section, highlight all countries that you want to ship to, internationally and domestically. If a country is not highlighted here, but is enabled in Zonos, the order will be allowed to complete and will appear in Dashboard, but it will fail to import to Magento.
  3. Select every country except for the United States in the Zip/postal code is optional for section. This reduces errors in completing orders since some countries do not use or require postal codes. Additionally, because Checkout comes with Zonos' fraud protection, you will not incur additional risk for removing this requirement.

⚖️ Add physical weight to Shopify SKUs

rawWidgets.content:
Zonos uses the physical weight value set at the product level to calculate the shipping costs of a shipment. To ensure carrier-calculated shipping rates are accurate in the checkout, we recommend adding a physical weight value to all active Shopify SKUs. 

Active SKUs that are missing a physical weight value in the product detail will rate carrier-calcualted shipping rates inaccurately.

taskTemplate.name: ⚖️ Add physical weight to Shopify SKUs
⚖️ Add physical weight to Shopify SKUs

rawWidgets.content:
How to Edit and Update Product Weights in Shopify

  1. In your Shopify admin, go to Products -> All products.
  2. Click the title of the product that you want to add weight to.
  3. Scroll down to the Shipping section.
    1. If the product has variants, you'll need to click one of the variants in the Variant section to see the Shipping section.
  4. Enter the weight under Weight.
  5. Click Save.

Instructions for creating custom messages

  1. From your Zonos dashboard, go to settings > custom messages (under checkout)
  2. Click Add custom message
  3. Select page (location of messages appear on the left, unless default, which displays on the right)
    1. Default - displays on every checkout page
    2. Customer information
    3. Shipping
    4. Payment
  4. Add custom message
  5. Select country/countries
  6. Save

Possible use-cases

  • Display link for domestic checkout with custom messages for domestic customers incorrectly directed to Zonos checkout
  • Setting customer expectation
  • Explaining return policies

Managing message translations

When you create a custom message, it will be automatically translated into all supported languages.

Once you have created a custom message, it will go into a translation queue and be translated within a few minutes. The message will be grayed-out in the list until the translation is complete. To view the translated version of the message, hover over the message to reveal the More options (•••) menu, then select View translations.

If you wish to decline any of the automatic translations, you can click the "thumbs down" button next to the translation. This will remove the translation from being displayed as well as providing feedback to our translation system.

A commercial invoice for Mexico must include the recipient’s RFC (Registro Federal de Contribuyentes) in the tax ID field, as it is required for customs clearance.

Enabling US sales tax requires two API calls

  1. Query for billingCompanyId
  2. Mutation to update merchant of record

Query for billingCompanyId

  • Open explore.zonos.com
  • Change store to your merchant's Zonos account
  • Copy their organizationId from the top of the screen
    • Top center of explore shows merchant name, merchant ID, and organizationId
  • Add organizationId to query below and run to obtain the billingCompanyId

{
  billingCompanyByOrganizationId(
    organizationId: "organizationId"
  ) {
    companyId
    name
    accounts {
      id
      type
    }
    country
    email
    id
    name
    createdAt
    createdBy
    id
    organizations {
      id
      name
    }
  }
}

Mutation to update merchant of record

  • Update mutation below with billingCompanyId obtained in the query above, countryCode (where they're based), and set ZONOS as the merchantOfRecord (both fields)
  • Run mutation

mutation MoRUpdate {
  billingCompanyUpdate(
    input: {
      id: "billingCompanyId"
      merchantOfRecordOverrides: {
        countryCode: US
        merchantOfRecord: ZONOS
      }
      merchantOfRecord: ZONOS
    }
  ) {
    merchantOfRecordOverrides {
      countryCode
      merchantOfRecord
    }
    merchantOfRecord
  }
}

taskTemplate.name: Add zx prefix to order number

rawWidgets.content:
Adding zx upstream

If you can add the zx prefix upstream (before orders reach ShipStation), you only need to configure your Label Messages to display the zx order number:

Label Messages configuration

  1. Access Label Messages settings: Navigate to Settings → Printing → Printing Setup, then click Document Options for Label
  2. Configure Label Messages: In the Label Messages section, you can define up to 3 custom messages that will appear as reference numbers on UPS labels. Label Messages only accept preset values like "Custom Field 1", "Order #", etc.
  3. Set your Messages to use Custom Fields: Set Message #1 to [Order #]. If you already have a #1, add a #2 or #3
  4. Save Settings to apply your configuration Once complete, ShipStation wil automatically put your order number on your label, which includes your configured zx prefix.

Adding zx in ShipStation:

If you can't add the zx prefix upstream (before orders reach ShipStation), you'll need to configure your Label Messages and Automation Rules to display the zx order number:

Step 1: Label Messages configuration

First, configure your Label Messages to use Custom Fields that will be populated by automation rules:

  1. Access Label Messages settings: Navigate to Settings → Printing → Printing Setup, then click Document Options for Label
  2. Configure Label Messages: In the Label Messages section, you can define up to 3 custom messages that will appear as reference numbers on UPS labels. Label Messages only accept preset values like "Custom Field 1", "Custom Field 2", etc.
  3. Set your Messages to use Custom Fields: Set Message #1 to [Custom Field #1]. If you already have a #1, add a #2 or #3
  4. Save Settings to apply your configuration

Automation Rules configuration

After setting up your new Label Message, you'll need an automation rule to populate your Custom Fields with the zx prefix and order data. Navigate to Settings → Automation → Automation Rules then click Create a Rule. Rule for Zonos UPS orders:

  1. Name: "Zonos UPS Unlock"
  2. Ensure Active Rule is toggled on
  3. Select Orders match these specific criteria
  4. Set criteria: "International Order" = "True" AND "Carrier" + "Includes" + "UPS"
  5. Set actions:
    • Action Type: Select "Set Custom Field 1..." (or 2 or 3 depending on your Label Messages)
    • Custom Field 1 (or 2 or 3): zx[order #] (this combines the zx prefix with the order number)
  6. Click Save Rule Once complete, ShipStation will automatically populate your Custom Field with the zx prefix and order number for international UPS orders, which will appear on your UPS labels. Important: The Label Message configuration in ShipStation is a global change that impacts all carriers. The automation rules approach allows UPS Unlock to work seamlessly for Zonos orders while maintaining your preferred Label Messages for other shipments.

  landedCostSettings {
    organization
    landedCostGuarantee
    disableDiscountedFee
    createdAt
    createdBy
    defaultCountryOfOrigin
    defaultCustomsDescription
    defaultHarmonizedCode
    minimumHsCodeLength
    mode
  }
}
Run the mutation below to enable LCG fees

mutation enableLcgDiscountFess {

  updateLandedCostSettings(input: {disableDiscountedFee: false}) {

    id

  }

}

config.plainTextBody:
Hey {{form.Integration_POC_First_Name}},

Congratulations on completing your Zonos integration! We're excited for you and are here to support you every step of your journey.

Zonos Support Team: 
Our dedicated support team will be monitoring your account closely during the initial phase. Should you require assistance, they are just an email away at support@zonos.com.

International Shipping:
To ensure a smooth experience for your international customers, please make sure to inform them that duty and tax will be collected at checkout. Additional resources below.
https://zonos.com/docs/guides/country-guides
https://zonos.com/docs/global-ecommerce/shipping-labels/create-a-label

Shopify Review: 
Your feedback means a lot to us! If you feel that your experience was worthy of a 5-star review, we'd love to get your insights via the link below: 
https://apps.shopify.com/duty-and-tax-calculator-iglobal-stores/reviews
If there was anything you feel we could have done to improve the experience for you, please share via email - onboarding@zonos.com

It has been a pleasure to work with you! Should you have any questions or need further assistance, please do not hesitate to reach out to us. We look forward to supporting your success with Zonos.

P.S. Want to announce your website’s new features to your customers? Below is a kit to help you!
https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1zXsD6SJ8lcHTkrdjb3W9x1qHfOY1768T
